text.title: Capacity Safety-Floor Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Capacity Safety-Floor Policy` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each service MUST have a pinned safety-floor (target utilisation %); `we'll see` is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a pinned floor there is no test for breach.

rule.statement: Floor breach for > 10 minutes MUST trigger a paging alert; ticket-only is forbidden for production.
rule.rationale: Breach without paging gets swept under low-priority queues.

rule.statement: Policy MUST be audited on a published cadence (≤ quarterly) against the prior cycle's breach history.
rule.rationale: Static floors drift from real traffic shape; review keeps them honest.

rule.statement: Each policy entry MUST list a named human owner who approves changes.
rule.rationale: Anonymous policies rot; named owner forces review.

rule.statement: Each policy MUST list a written breach-response runbook link (immediate scale-out / shed-load / promote canary).
rule.rationale: Floor breach without a documented response is a postmortem waiting to happen.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
